source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, it is known that the length of the fishing rod $AC$ is $10\mathrm{m}$, and the length of the fishing line $BC$ exposed above the water surface is $6\mathrm{m}$. A fisher wants to check the situation on the hook, so he rotates the fishing rod $AC$ to the position $A{C}^{\prime}$, at this time, the length of the fishing line ${B}^{\prime}{C}^{\prime}$ exposed above the water surface is $8\mathrm{m}$. What is the length of $B{B}^{\prime}$?

issues: metadata.image_paths does not match the top-level image_paths value.

image_dependency: question begins with 'As shown in the figure', indicating explicit reliance on the diagram
the provided solution uses geometric facts from the figure such as angle ABC=90° and angle AB'C'=90° that are not fully specified in text alone
multi_step: solution requires computing AB from AC and BC, then computing AB' from AC' and B'C', then subtracting to get BB'
the task combines textual quantities with diagram-based geometric structure
there are at least two dependent reasoning steps before the final numeric answer
verifiability: final answer is a single numeric length (2 m), which is easy to check
the provided solution shows explicit intermediate formulas based on the Pythagorean theorem
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and both question and answer are present

risk_flags: metadata.image_paths does not match the top-level image_paths value.

decision_reasons: Question, answer, and primary image are all present, so the core semantic assets are available.
Initial scoring is strong: high image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability.
The image exists and the text/answer lengths indicate substantial recoverable content.
Recorded issues are metadata inconsistencies rather than loss of core problem content.

normalization_record.normalized_question_text: As shown in the figure, it is known that the length of the fishing rod $AC$ is $10\mathrm{m}$, and the length of the fishing line $BC$ exposed above the water surface is $6\mathrm{m}$. A fisher wants to check the situation on the hook, so he rotates the fishing rod $AC$ to the position $AC'$, at this time, the length of the fishing line $B'C'$ exposed above the water surface is $8\mathrm{m}$. What is the length of $BB'$?
normalization_record.normalized_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Given $\because AC=10\text{ m},\ BC=6\text{ m},\ \angle ABC=90^\circ$, \\
$\therefore AB=\sqrt{AC^{2}-BC^{2}}=\sqrt{10^{2}-6^{2}}=8\text{ m}$, \\
$\because AC'=10\text{ m},\ B'C'=8\text{ m},\ \angle AB'C'=90^\circ$, \\
$\therefore AB'=\sqrt{AC'^{2}-B'C'^{2}}=\sqrt{10^{2}-8^{2}}=6\text{ m}$, \\
$\therefore BB'=AB-AB'=\boxed{2}\text{ m}.$
normalization_notes: Key geometric relations used in the solution, including right angles at $B$ and $B'$, depend on the diagram rather than text alone.

clean_problem_record.normalized_question_text: As shown in the figure, it is known that the length of the fishing rod $AC$ is $10\mathrm{m}$, and the length of the fishing line $BC$ exposed above the water surface is $6\mathrm{m}$. A fisher wants to check the situation on the hook, so he rotates the fishing rod $AC$ to the position $AC'$, at this time, the length of the fishing line $B'C'$ exposed above the water surface is $8\mathrm{m}$. What is the length of $BB'$?
clean_problem_record.normalized_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Given $\because AC=10\text{ m},\ BC=6\text{ m},\ \angle ABC=90^\circ$, \\
$\therefore AB=\sqrt{AC^{2}-BC^{2}}=\sqrt{10^{2}-6^{2}}=8\text{ m}$, \\
$\because AC'=10\text{ m},\ B'C'=8\text{ m},\ \angle AB'C'=90^\circ$, \\
$\therefore AB'=\sqrt{AC'^{2}-B'C'^{2}}=\sqrt{10^{2}-8^{2}}=6\text{ m}$, \\
$\therefore BB'=AB-AB'=\boxed{2}\text{ m}.$
